desc: Sapporo is a spacious, grid-planned northern city that works both as Hokkaido's urban anchor and as a base for snow, food and seasonal festivals: beer halls, ramen lanes, seafood markets, broad boulevards and easy rail access sit beside one of Japan's most famous winter-event calendars. It suits travellers who want Japan with more breathing room, colder seasons and stronger outdoor identity than Tokyo or Osaka.

todo.name: Odori Park and TV Tower
todo.desc: Use the central park strip to understand Sapporo's grid and seasonal festival layout.

todo.name: Sapporo Beer Museum
todo.desc: Pair beer history with a jingisukan lamb meal for a classic city stop.

todo.name: Nijo Market
todo.desc: Go in the morning for seafood bowls and Hokkaido produce.

todo.name: Otaru or ski day trip
todo.desc: Use rail for canal-town Otaru or winter connections toward nearby ski areas.

todo.name: Susukino evening food crawl
todo.desc: Give the city a proper evening slot for ramen, jingisukan, bars and the brighter side of Sapporo after dark.

todo.name: Seasonal Odori event timing
todo.desc: Check what is actually on in Odori Park before you go, because Sapporo changes character a lot between Snow Festival, lilac season, beer garden and autumn food events.

wifi.note: Excellent across stations, business hotels and city cafes, so Sapporo is easy to navigate and re-plan from on the fly; the bigger issue is winter weather, not connectivity.

prac.fltNote: New Chitose is the real gateway for Sapporo and Hokkaido trips, while Okadama is a much more minor domestic airport. From the UK, most itineraries still connect through Tokyo or other Asian hubs rather than flying directly into Hokkaido, and the city can feel expensive and capacity-tight when Snow Festival dates, ski traffic and holiday demand all pile into the same winter window.
prac.lang: Japanese; English support varies by city and transport hub
prac.visa: British citizens can enter Japan for tourism or business for up to 90 days on arrival, and GOV.UK says your passport only needs to be valid for the length of your stay, with a blank page for the entry stamp.
alerts: Snow Festival dates sell out early.
Winter storms can delay flights and trains.
Restaurant queues are common for famous ramen and seafood spots.
Do not underestimate how spread-out Hokkaido add-ons can be, even if Sapporo itself feels easy.

sweet: February for the full snow-city version, or June to September for cooler-weather city breaks, are usually Sapporo's strongest windows depending on whether you want festival winter or easy Hokkaido summer movement.
